1.texto_original: OR quant’è a’ figliuoli d’Aaronne, i loro spartimenti furono questi: I figliuoli d’Aaronne furono Nadab, ed Abihu, Eleazaro, ed Itamar.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of 1 Cronache 24:1

"OR quant’è a’ figliuoli d’Aaronne, i loro spartimenti furono questi: I figliuoli d’Aaronne furono Nadab, ed Abihu, Eleazaro, ed Itamar."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 1 Cronache 24:1

Philological Analysis

"OR quant’è a’ figliuoli d’Aaronne, i loro spartimenti furono questi: I figliuoli d’Aaronne furono Nadab, ed Abihu, Eleazaro, ed Itamar."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"OR quant’è a’ figliuoli d’Aaronne, i loro spartimenti furono questi: I figliuoli d’Aaronne furono Nadab, ed Abihu, Eleazaro, ed Itamar."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

6.texto_original: E Semaia, figliuolo di Natanael, Scriba, della tribù di Levi, li descrisse davanti al re, e davanti a’ capi del popolo, ed al sacerdote Sadoc, e ad Ahimelec, figliuolo di Ebiatar, ed a’ capi delle famiglie paterne de’ sacerdoti, e de’ Leviti; una casa paterna si prendeva de’ discendenti di Eleazaro, ed un’altra congiuntamente di que’ d’Itamar.
6.normal:
Devotional Study of 1 Cronache 24:6

"E Semaia, figliuolo di Natanael, Scriba, della tribù di Levi, li descrisse davanti al re, e davanti a’ capi del popolo, ed al sacerdote Sadoc, e ad Ahimelec, figliuolo di Ebiatar, ed a’ capi delle famiglie paterne de’ sacerdoti, e de’ Leviti; una casa paterna si prendeva de’ discendenti di Eleazaro, ed un’altra congiuntamente di que’ d’Itamar."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.

31.texto_original: E tirarono anch’essi le sorti, al pari de’ figliuoli d’Aaronne, lor fratelli, in presenza del re Davide, e di Sadoc, e d’Ahimelec, e de’ capi delle famiglie paterne de’ sacerdoti, e de’ Leviti; le principali delle case paterne essendo pareggiate ad altre più piccole de’ lor fratelli.
31.normal:
Devotional Study of 1 Cronache 24:31

"E tirarono anch’essi le sorti, al pari de’ figliuoli d’Aaronne, lor fratelli, in presenza del re Davide, e di Sadoc, e d’Ahimelec, e de’ capi delle famiglie paterne de’ sacerdoti, e de’ Leviti; le principali delle case paterne essendo pareggiate ad altre più piccole de’ lor fratelli."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
31.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 1 Cronache 24:31

Philological Analysis

"E tirarono anch’essi le sorti, al pari de’ figliuoli d’Aaronne, lor fratelli, in presenza del re Davide, e di Sadoc, e d’Ahimelec, e de’ capi delle famiglie paterne de’ sacerdoti, e de’ Leviti; le principali delle case paterne essendo pareggiate ad altre più piccole de’ lor fratelli."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
31.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"E tirarono anch’essi le sorti, al pari de’ figliuoli d’Aaronne, lor fratelli, in presenza del re Davide, e di Sadoc, e d’Ahimelec, e de’ capi delle famiglie paterne de’ sacerdoti, e de’ Leviti; le principali delle case paterne essendo pareggiate ad altre più piccole de’ lor fratelli."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️
